meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solving Marketing Gaps
content.seo_description: Learn how to use project management to overcome marketing hurdles. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your SaaS.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree; you need a system. By treating your product roadmap as a project management task, you prioritize features that sell themselves. Using Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to execute, you turn technical building into a marketing-focused, high-conversion development process.
content.intro:
The code is written, the interface is polished, and then silence. You launch to an empty room because 'marketing' feels like a black box you never studied. This is the classic developer's trap: believing that great software sells itself without a structured bridge between logic and user needs.

But what if your project management was your marketing? By integrating product-led growth into your build phase, you stop chasing trends and start solving user problems that generate their own momentum. This case study shows you how to leverage AI tools to transform your development cycle into a high-visibility, user-focused roadmap.

steps.title: Define User-Centric Milestones
steps.description: Shift your focus from 'features' to 'outcomes'. Identify the specific problem your tool solves to ensure every line of code serves a marketing goal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Manager. Analyze my core feature set and map them to 5 distinct user outcomes that solve a specific pain point for beginners.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a project roadmap based on user outcomes'

steps.title: Architecting for Visibility
steps.description: Structure your codebase to support 'shareable' moments, like progress reports or exported data, which act as organic marketing assets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current architecture to include 'social hooks' where users can export their project milestones as a shareable image or summary.

steps.title: Automating Feedback Loops
steps.description: Implement a lightweight feedback loop within your app so you can gather testimonials and feature requests while the user is actively engaged.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a non-intrusive feedback widget that triggers after a successful task completion and stores results in a JSON file.

steps.title: Documentation as Content
steps.description: Turn your internal documentation into public-facing 'Build Logs'. This builds trust and attracts early adopters who value transparency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that parses my daily commits and generates a professional 'Dev Log' summary in Markdown format for my landing page.

steps.title: Deployment and Launch Readiness
steps.description: Package your app with a 'marketing-first' deployment script that pre-configures your metadata for search engines and social previews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SEO-optimized deployment configuration that includes OpenGraph meta tags and a sitemap for better search engine indexing.

pro_tips: Treat your GitHub README as your primary landing page; developers are your first users.
Use 'Build in Public' as a project management strategy to keep yourself accountable to your roadmap.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a single, burning problem rather than building a bloated, feature-rich app.
Automate your social media updates directly from your commit messages using simple CI/CD triggers.
Always include a 'Request a Feature' button in your MVP; it makes users feel invested in your success.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share your project link in three relevant developer communities and iterate based on the feedback loop you built in Step 3.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the technical edge to build a product that stands out in a crowded market. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' time and start shipping your vision today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
